raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing this complaint because Barclays failed to conduct a reasonable investigation of my disputed charge and refused to address clear inconsistencies in the merchants documentation. 

The moving companys final invoice contains timestamp discrepancies showing signatures recorded minutes apart at a time when I was not physically present with the mover. This suggests the paperwork was completed or altered after the fact. The charges on the invoice also do not match what was agreed upon at the time of service. I submitted these discrepancies to Barclays as part of my dispute. 

Barclays did not address the timestamp inconsistencies, the altered invoice, or how they reached their conclusion. Instead, they provided a generic explanation that did not answer my questions and did not evaluate the disputed documents. 

After receiving Barclays response, I contacted the Office of the President at the phone number provided in their letter. I reiterated that the documents are inconsistent and unreliable. I was told that Barclays could not do anything further, even though they never addressed the core evidence or explained what materials they relied on.

I am requesting that the CFPB direct Barclays to conduct a reasonable reinvestigation as required by the Fair Credit Billing Act, and provide a clear explanation of how they reached their conclusion given that the merchants documents contain factual discrepancies.
